description: Long-term architecture stabilization by tightening boundaries, ownership, configuration contracts, and executable guardrails.

userStories.title: Remove tracked generated artifacts
userStories.description: As a maintainer, I need generated package metadata and unused starter assets out of source control.
acceptanceCriteria: `git grep -n -I -e "hero.png" -e "react.svg" -e "vite.svg" -- apps/web/src index.html` returns no matches

userStories.title: Enforce frontend config contract
userStories.description: As a maintainer, I need npm, typecheck, OpenAPI, and Vite proxy drift to fail fast.

userStories.title: Consolidate browser preference transport
userStories.description: As a developer, I need backend client preferences to have one cache and migration primitive.
acceptanceCriteria: `apps/web/src/shared/preferences/clientPreferences.ts` exposes cache status and optimistic save helpers
`migrateLocalPreferenceToBackend` does not delete legacy localStorage when backend loading fails
`migrateLocalPreferenceToBackend` uploads legacy localStorage when backend key is null or missing
`apps/web/src/shared/preferences/clientPreferences.test.ts` covers missing-backend migration and offline preservation

userStories.title: Create persistent preference store primitive
userStories.description: As a developer, I need reusable preference stores to share backend-first read, optimistic write, and domain event behavior.
acceptanceCriteria: `apps/web/src/shared/preferences/persistentPreferenceStore.ts` contains the generic store primitive only
Store read prefers loaded backend cache over stale localStorage
Store write does not create a long-lived localStorage shadow copy
`apps/web/src/shared/preferences/persistentPreferenceStore.test.ts` covers backend-first read and optimistic update events

userStories.title: Move cross-feature preference models to entities
userStories.description: As a frontend maintainer, I need shared preference schemas to have one entity-level owner.
acceptanceCriteria: `rg "features/.*/.*Settings" apps/web/src/entities apps/web/src/features` shows no imports from deleted feature-owned settings files

userStories.title: Extract shortcut binding kernel
userStories.description: As a developer, I need shortcut capture, labels, signatures, validation, and matching to share one implementation.
acceptanceCriteria: English and global shortcut modules declare action schemas and defaults only
`apps/web/src/shared/keyboard/shortcutBindings.test.ts` covers capture, labels, signatures, and modifier matching

userStories.title: Migrate review feedback preference to backend authority
userStories.description: As a maintainer, I need review feedback settings to stop treating localStorage as a second source of truth.
acceptanceCriteria: `readReviewFeedbackSettings` reads loaded client-preference cache before localStorage

userStories.title: Migrate timer preferences to backend authority
userStories.description: As a maintainer, I need timer automation and focus settings to follow the same backend-first preference model.
acceptanceCriteria: `readTimerAutomationConfig` and `readTimerFocusConfig` read loaded client-preference cache before localStorage
`saveTimerAutomationConfig` and `saveTimerFocusConfig` call `saveClientPreference` and do not call `localStorage.setItem`
Reset functions remove legacy localStorage keys only as cleanup

userStories.title: Keep route-local preference hook backend-first
userStories.description: As a frontend maintainer, I need route-local preference state to bridge to backend preferences without hard-coded shared key maps.
acceptanceCriteria: `apps/web/src/shared/lib/localStorage.ts` accepts explicit preference ownership instead of hard-coded dashboard/palace key maps
Feature callers provide backend preference keys explicitly; no local preference registry exists
`useLocalStorageState` still listens to `CLIENT_PREFERENCES_UPDATED_EVENT` for mapped keys
Dashboard, palace list, and palace shelf pages still typecheck
`python tools/check_architecture.py` rejects reintroducing the local preference registry

userStories.title: Keep Dashboard implementation under features
userStories.description: As a frontend maintainer, I need the Dashboard business page to live in feature-owned code and not in app/router.
acceptanceCriteria: `apps/web/src/app/router` no longer contains `DashboardPage.tsx` or its dashboard tests
Route wiring imports the feature page instead of hosting the implementation
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Keep palace catalog implementation under features
userStories.description: As a frontend maintainer, I need palace list and shelf business pages plus their catalog model to live in feature-owned code.
acceptanceCriteria: Palace list and shelf implementations live in `apps/web/src/features/palace-catalog`
Catalog scope and flattening logic live in a feature model module
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Extract palace quiz title read contract
userStories.description: As a backend maintainer, I need palace_quiz to read palace titles through a stable contract instead of palaces internals.
acceptanceCriteria: A public application contract or projection exposes only required title/chapter data

userStories.title: Extract mindmap model serialization contract
userStories.description: As a backend maintainer, I need quiz generation to use a public mindmap serialization contract.
acceptanceCriteria: New contract tests cover required mindmap input/output conversion

userStories.title: Extract quiz grouping read projections
userStories.description: As a backend maintainer, I need quiz grouping to depend on public mini-palace and segment projections.
acceptanceCriteria: Projection module exposes only data required by quiz grouping

userStories.title: Guard palace_quiz application imports
userStories.description: As a maintainer, I need architecture checks to prevent new palace_quiz imports from palaces private application internals.
acceptanceCriteria: Imports from `memory_anki.modules.palaces.application.*` fail unless they target an approved public contract
Adding an illegal import locally makes `python tools/check_architecture.py` fail
`python tools/check_architecture.py` passes on the current code

userStories.title: Split PalaceQuizGenerationPanel
userStories.description: As a frontend maintainer, I need quiz generation UI split by responsibility.
acceptanceCriteria: Input, progress, history, and container responsibilities live in separate files
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Split useTimedSession
userStories.description: As a frontend maintainer, I need timed session storage, snapshot, restore, and browser effects separated.
acceptanceCriteria: Storage, restoration, snapshot, and browser side effects live in separate modules
Pure model tests cover snapshot and restore boundaries

userStories.title: Split English reading workflow state model
userStories.description: As a frontend maintainer, I need English reading state transitions separated from asynchronous orchestration.
acceptanceCriteria: Core state transitions live in a testable reducer or model module
EnglishReadingPage core, interaction, and session tests pass
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Move review API wrappers to review feature
userStories.description: As a frontend maintainer, I need review endpoints to be owned by the review feature rather than shared business API modules.
acceptanceCriteria: `apps/web/src/features/review/api/reviewApi.ts` owns review, segment-review, mini-review, and review progress wrappers

userStories.title: Move runtime and AI log APIs to entity owners
userStories.description: As a frontend maintainer, I need cross-feature runtime and AI log endpoints to be entity-owned instead of broad shared API modules.
acceptanceCriteria: `python tools/check_architecture.py` rejects recreating those shared API modules

userStories.title: Move knowledge API wrappers to knowledge entity
userStories.description: As a frontend maintainer, I need subject, chapter, editor, and document endpoints to be owned by the knowledge entity.
acceptanceCriteria: `apps/web/src/entities/knowledge/api/knowledgeApi.ts` owns subject, chapter, subject-editor, and subject-document wrappers

userStories.title: Move quiz API wrappers to quiz entity
userStories.description: As a frontend maintainer, I need question and quiz-generation endpoints to be entity-owned so knowledge and palace quiz features do not depend on each other.
acceptanceCriteria: `apps/web/src/entities/quiz/api/quizApi.ts` owns quiz question, chapter quiz, generation, recovery, classification, attempt, and feedback wrappers
`tools/check_architecture.py` rejects recreating the removed shared or feature quiz API files

userStories.title: Move palace and import APIs to entity owners
userStories.description: As a frontend maintainer, I need palace APIs and import-job APIs out of the shared palaces business bucket and owned by their domains.
acceptanceCriteria: `apps/web/src/entities/palace/api` owns palace catalog, editor, practice, version, chapter-link, attachment, focus, and palace state API wrappers
`apps/web/src/entities/knowledge-import/api` owns import preview, stream parsing, and import-job API wrappers
`apps/web/src/features/mindmap-import` owns shared mind-map import workflow UI, hooks, and model
`tools/check_architecture.py` rejects recreating the removed shared palaces module or directory

userStories.title: Document architecture guardrails
userStories.description: As a maintainer using AI coding tools, I need concise guardrails that preserve boundaries without freezing evolution.
acceptanceCriteria: `docs/architecture/README.md` documents backend ownership and allowed cross-module dependencies
`docs/architecture/README.md` documents preference ownership and backend client preferences authority
`docs/architecture/README.md` documents config contract files and generated-output cleanup rules
A short AI architecture prompt is present
